fix_plans.title: Extend training to 200 epochs
fix_plans.content: File: train.py. Edit: in build_parser() change the --train-epochs argparse default from 150 to 200 (parser.add_argument("--train-epochs", type=int, default=200)). Leave everything else verbatim: n_hidden=256, n_layers=2 (hard-coded in main()'s SCVI constructor), adversarial_classifier=True, scale_adversarial_loss=2.0, n_epochs_kl_warmup=20, lr=1e-3, dropout_rate=0.1, n_latent=10, batch_balance_strength=1.0, max_cells=8000, batch_size=128, the sc.pp.highly_variable_genes(...) call in build_dataset(), the AdversarialTrainingPlan monkey-patch, _ProbeCallback, the ANCHOR markers, and the prober_conclude(0.80, 0.55, anchors=_anchors) call. Rationale: round 9 already crossed the 0.80 standard threshold (tail_mean 0.801, final 0.799, peak 0.833 at epoch 73) using n_hidden=256/n_layers=2/150 epochs/adversarial+KL-warmup, and the kBET trajectory from epoch 100 onward oscillates in the 0.79-0.83 band rather than plateauing — the encoder is still gradually improving batch mixing with more updates. Giving the adversarial head 50 more epochs under sustained scale_adversarial_loss=2.0 pressure (which does not decay, unlike 'auto') will push the tail mean of the last 5 epochs further above 0.80 by averaging over a longer converged band, tipping the marginal 0.001 shortfall over the threshold. ELBO anchor is preserved: the ELBO at epoch 150 has already largely converged (elbo_validation 4888, elbo_train 4546), so 50 more epochs will not degrade reconstruction by more than a couple percent and the utility-floor guard stays safe. No risk of undertraining or cutting the learning rate.

fix_plans.title: Widen encoder hidden to 320
fix_plans.content: File: train.py. Edit: in build_parser() change the --n-hidden argparse default from 256 to 320 (parser.add_argument("--n-hidden", type=int, default=320)). Keep --train-epochs at 150, n_layers=2 inline in the SCVI constructor, adversarial_classifier=True, scale_adversarial_loss=2.0, n_epochs_kl_warmup=20, lr=1e-3, dropout_rate=0.1, n_latent=10, batch_balance_strength=1.0, max_cells=8000, the seurat_v3 HVG call, and the AdversarialTrainingPlan monkey-patch. Rationale: the encoder is currently 8000->256->256->256->10 with n_layers=2 and n_hidden=256. kBET has been climbing monotonically with every capacity/depth increase (rounds 2, 6, 9) and is now bottlenecked on the encoder's ability to simultaneously fit the rich PBMC expression manifold and align the two batch distributions. Widening n_hidden 256->320 (~25% more capacity per layer, ~1.4x total parameters) gives the adversarial batch classifier a richer, more batch-invariant representation to work against and a larger latent manifold to mix batches in. This is the model-capacity angle, distinct from the training-length angle of plan 1. ELBO anchor will very likely DROP (better reconstruction fit), so the utility-floor guard is not at risk; the adversarial loss slightly slows convergence but 150 epochs is plenty for n_hidden=320 on 8000 cells. No other files modified; record()/conclude() and prober.py untouched.

fix_plans.title: Raise adversarial loss weight to 3.0
fix_plans.content: File: train.py. Edit: in main()'s vae.train(...) plan_kwargs dict, change "scale_adversarial_loss": 2.0 to "scale_adversarial_loss": 3.0. Keep adversarial_classifier=True, n_epochs_kl_warmup=20, lr=1e-3, --train-epochs=150, --n-hidden=256, n_layers=2 inline, dropout_rate=0.1, n_latent=10, batch_balance_strength=1.0, max_cells=8000, the seurat_v3 HVG call, and the AdversarialTrainingPlan monkey-patch. Rationale: round 9's per-batch kBET acceptance is still 0.0/0.0 even though the global kBET tail reached 0.801 — meaning within each batch's own neighbourhood the batch composition is still skewed, and the encoder is only mixing batches at the global subsample level, not at the local neighbourhood level that kBET actually measures. scale_adversarial_loss=2.0 has been the constant since round 5 (which lifted kBET from 0.5455 to 0.619) and is now the limiting lever: increasing the adversarial gradient pressure on the encoder to ~1.5x will penalize batch-predictable latents harder and force the per-batch neighbourhood composition to approach the global mix, which is exactly the per-batch acceptance the probe is reporting as 0.0/0.0. This is the optimization-loss-weight angle, distinct from capacity (plan 2) and training length (plan 1). ELBO anchor will rise modestly (heavier adversarial loss slows reconstruction) but by well under the 20% utility-floor threshold given round 9's elbo_validation 4888 has substantial margin; the KL warmup of 20 epochs already shields reconstruction early. No other files modified; record()/conclude() and prober.py untouched.
